methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ACSL, a Tokyo-listed public company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Satoshi Washiya, presents a challenging valuation environment due to the nascent and fragmented nature of its peer universe. The sector comprises approximately five same-sector entities, though no established public comparables exist in this space. The relevant private peer set includes five leading companies: Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, all operating across adjacent autonomous applications.

Standard peer-median analysis is not applicable here. The Drones & Autonomous sector lacks sufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers to support robust triangulation—a minimum of three datapoints is required for reliable median derivation, but the narrow filters yielded zero matches across all three dimensions. Falling back to an all-sector median would introduce material distortion, as the private peer set operates across heterogeneous business models, geographies, and funding stages.

This analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without additional data on comparable transaction multiples, discounted cash flow inputs, or regulatory/commercialization milestones specific to ACSL's business. Key uncertainties include the company's path to profitability, regulatory approval timelines for autonomous operations in Japan, and the ultimate margin structure of drone services relative to capital requirements. A complete valuation would require either narrower peer cohort data or company-specific financial metrics and forward projections.
